issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global focus-visible style removes the default browser outline but does not provide a visible replacement such as a box-shadow or a new outline color. This prevents keyboard users from identifying which element currently has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 245: :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-brand-primary); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: focus indicator removed in high-contrast mode
issuesFound.explanation: The forced-colors media query explicitly removes the focus outline. In high-contrast mode, background colors and box-shadows are often stripped, making the outline the only reliable way to perceive focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 258: @media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }

issuesFound.title: visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses static positioning and auto dimensions, which fails to hide content visually. This results in "hidden" labels appearing on screen and potentially clashing with other layout elements while failing the standard accessible hiding pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 276: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: Setting a negative word-spacing on the body element causes words to crowd together, which significantly impairs readability for users with low vision or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 185: word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: negative letter-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The hero heading uses a negative letter-spacing value. Tightening the space between characters makes text harder to decode, especially for users with dyslexia or visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 715: letter-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: insufficient colour contrast
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses a foreground color (#ffffff) on a background color (#5588bb), resulting in a contrast ratio of 2.8:1. This is below the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 311: background-color: var(--colour-neutral-800); color: var(--colour-neutral-0);

issuesFound.title: root font size below minimum readability threshold
issuesFound.explanation: The root font size is set to 12px. Since all other sizes (rem) are calculated from this base, the resulting body text and UI elements will be significantly smaller than the standard 16px, causing significant strain for users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 168: font-size: 16px;

issuesFound.title: toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions toggle CSS classes and scroll-lock state but fail to update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be notified that the navigation drawer has been deployed or retracted.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1455: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
Line 1474: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing toggle switch (role="switch") updates its visual track state but does not update the aria-checked attribute to reflect whether annual billing is active. This violates the requirement for switch widgets to communicate their binary state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2330: setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.CHECKED, isYear);

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links an error message to a field via aria-describedby but fails to set the aria-invalid attribute to true. Screen readers will not announce the field as being in an error state when focus lands on it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1387: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the error reference from aria-describedby but does not reset the aria-invalid attribute. A field previously marked as invalid will continue to be announced as such even after the user corrects the input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1397: setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialisation function identifies the trigger button but does not set an initial aria-expanded state. Interactive disclosure triggers must have an explicit state on load to ensure predictable behavior for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1515: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: Toggle function for mobile navigation does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and mobileNav.close() functions change the visible state of the navigation drawer but do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed whether the navigation menu is expanded or collapsed, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2150: In the open() function, add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');  
Line 2160: In the close() function, add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Product filter view mode toggle does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter._onViewToggle() function changes view mode but does not update aria-pressed on the toggle buttons. Screen reader users cannot tell which view (grid/list) is active, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2550: In _onViewToggle(), after setting _productFilterState.viewMode, add: qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => b.setAttribute('aria-pressed', b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === mode ? 'true' : 'false'));

issuesFound.title: Dynamic product count update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product count badge but does not announce the change via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users may not know the filter results have changed, degrading experience per WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2500: In applyFilters(), after _updateCountBadge(totalMatches), add: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function marks fields as invalid visually but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input elements. Screen reader users will not be notified of validation errors, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3650: In _renderResult(), when result.valid is false, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); when valid, add: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on mobile navigation trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the navigation toggle button when the page loads with the menu closed. Screen reader users won't know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2180: In init(), after _trigger is assigned, add: _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on mobile navigation trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav open() and close() functions change the visual state of the navigation drawer but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen-reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1840: add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); after _open = true;
Line 1857: add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); after _open = false;

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on dropdown menu trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu _openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show/hide the dropdown panel but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. This breaks the ARIA disclosure pattern for screen‑reader users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1974: add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, true); after inst.open = true;
Line 1990: add setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false); after inst.open = false;

issuesFound.title: Billing period toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function switches the active billing period and updates CSS classes on the period buttons, but does not set aria-pressed to reflect the selected state. Screen‑reader users cannot perceive which period is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3404: Inside _syncToggleState, after toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); add setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);

issuesFound.title: Product filter tabs do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter _onTabClick() function changes the active filter tab and updates CSS classes, but does not set aria-pressed on the tab buttons. This prevents screen‑reader users from knowing which filter is selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2938: Inside _syncTabAriaStates, after toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); add setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, isActive);

issuesFound.title: Carousel slide change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel goTo() function changes the visible slide but does not update an ARIA live region to announce the change. Screen‑reader users are not informed when the carousel advances.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4246: After liveRegion.announce(...); add a second announcement that updates a dedicated live‑region element (e.g., document.getElementById('carousel-status').textContent = `Slide ${target + 1} of ${count}`;). Alternatively, ensure the liveRegion.announce call is made with a clear, descriptive message.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator _renderResult() function adds error classes and messages but does not set aria-invalid="true" on invalid fields. Screen readers will not announce the invalid state of the field.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 4678: add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, true); inside the invalid branch.
Line 4684: add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, false); inside the valid branch.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on mobile navigation trigger at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init() function does not set an initial aria-expanded value on the trigger button. If the menu is closed on load, the trigger must have aria-expanded="false" from the start.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1899: After _initialised = true; add if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on dropdown menu triggers at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu init() function registers dropdown triggers but does not set aria-expanded="false" on them initially. This violates the ARIA disclosure pattern requirement that the state must be present on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2050: Inside the register function, after setting aria-haspopup and aria-controls, add setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, false);

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce results via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters() function updates visible product count but does not announce this change via an ARIA live region. Screen reader users are not informed when filter results change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2645: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches) : \`${totalMatches} products found\`);` after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button when the menu is closed on page load. Screen reader users start with an undefined state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1825: Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');` after `_initialised = true;`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu init() function does not set aria-expanded="false" on dropdown trigger buttons when panels are closed on page load. Screen reader users start with undefined states.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1975: Add `_instances.forEach(inst => inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false'));` after `debug(\`dropdownMenu.init: ${_instances.length} dropdown(s) registered\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on global :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global `:focus-visible` rule removes the outline (`outline: none`) without providing any alternative visible focus style such as a box‑shadow or colored outline. Keyboard‑only users will not see a focus indicator on any interactive element, violating WCAG 2.4.7.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The rule `:focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: none; }` removes the default focus outline for keyboard users when the element does not match `:focus-visible`, and no replacement style is supplied.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons lose their outline on focus (`outline: none`) and no alternative visual cue is defined, making it impossible for keyboard users to locate the focused button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Links inside feature cards have `outline: none` on focus without any replacement, leaving no focus indicator for keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ toggle buttons remove the outline (`outline: none`) on focus and do not provide an alternative visual cue, breaking focus visibility for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes the outline on focus without any replacement, so keyboard users cannot see when the region has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes its outline on focus and provides no alternative visual indicator, hindering keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The utility class `.focus-ring-inset` removes the outline on focus without supplying any visible replacement, leaving focus invisible for elements that use this class.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: `.focus-ring-brand` sets only `outline-color` while the outline itself is removed (`outline: none` from the global rule), resulting in no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-sm` sets `min-height: 20px`, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum target size of 44 × 44 px, making the button difficult to activate for users with limited motor control.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: `.btn-icon` defines `min-width: 20px` and `min-height: 20px`, both below the required 44 px minimum, resulting in a target that is hard to tap.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: `.hero-image img` animates `transform` and `box-shadow` on hover, but there is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule that disables or simplifies this animation, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of `.hero-image img` changes `transform` and `box-shadow` without a reduced‑motion guard, causing motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Include the same reduced‑motion override as above (see Issue 12).

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button in the hero section moves upward (`transform: translateY(-1px)`) on hover, but no reduced‑motion media query disables this motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .pricing-card--featured:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card applies `transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01)` on hover, creating motion without a reduced‑motion fallback.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule `a { text-decoration: none; }` removes the default underline from all links, and no other visual differentiator (border, background, font‑weight) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for users relying on visual cues.

fpTitles: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .hero-image img:hover

issuesFound.title: Global focus-visible removes outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and provides no visible alternative (no box‑shadow, border, or colored outline). Keyboard‑only users cannot see which element receives focus.

issuesFound.title: .btn:focus-visible removes outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the outline (outline: none) and does not provide any other visible focus indicator, leaving button focus invisible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: .filter-tabs button:focus-visible removes outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button:focus-visible selector removes the outline and provides no alternative visual cue, making focus on filter buttons invisible.

issuesFound.title: .faq-item dt button:focus-visible removes outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button:focus-visible rule removes the outline without supplying any other visible focus styling, so focus on FAQ toggle buttons is not perceivable.

issuesFound.title: .nav-toggle:focus-visible removes outline without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector removes the outline and does not add any other visible focus indicator, leaving the mobile navigation toggle focus invisible.

issuesFound.title: .btn touch target below minimum size (height 32px)
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is smaller than the WCAG‑recommended 44 px touch target size, making buttons hard to tap for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: .btn-sm touch target below minimum size (height 20px)
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, far below the 44 px minimum, creating a very small tap area.

issuesFound.title: .btn-lg touch target below minimum size (height 36px)
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-lg class sets min-height: 36px, still below the 44 px recommended minimum for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: .btn-icon touch target below minimum size (20 px × 20 px)
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class defines min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is smaller than the 44 px minimum, making icon‑only buttons difficult to activate.

issuesFound.title: .nav-toggle touch target below minimum size (28 px × 28 px)
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle class sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, both below the 44 px minimum, reducing tapability of the mobile menu button.

issuesFound.title: .pricing-toggle button touch target below minimum size (height 28px)
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44 px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: .sr-only utility incorrectly hides content from AT
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static; width: auto; height: auto; and does not apply the required clipping technique, causing the element to remain visible to sighted users and not be reliably announced by assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: .btn transition includes transform without prefers‑reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class defines transition: … transform var(--transition-fast) which animates spatial movement, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override disabling this for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: .hero-image img transition includes transform without prefers‑reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector applies transition: transform var(--transition-slower) which animates movement, but no reduced‑motion media query disables it.

issuesFound.title: .hero-image img:hover transform animation without prefers‑reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img:hover rule animates transform (rotation) without a reduced‑motion guard, causing motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover transform animation without prefers‑reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-1px) which moves the button, but no reduced‑motion override is provided.

issuesFound.title: .pricing-card--featured:hover transform/scale animation without prefers‑reduced‑motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover rule animates transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) without a reduced‑motion guard, violating WCAG 2.3.3 for users preferring reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em; which compresses spacing between words, reducing readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative visual cue
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes underlines (text-decoration: none) and no other visual differentiator (e.g., color change, border) is provided, making links indistinguishable from surrounding text for users relying on visual cues.

.btn-lg touch target below minimum size (height 36px)
.hero-image img transition includes transform without prefers‑reduced‑motion override

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open()`, `mobileNav.close()` and `mobileNav.toggle()` functions change the visibility of the mobile navigation drawer but never set the `aria‑expanded` attribute on the trigger element (`SELECTORS.navToggle`). Screen‑reader users therefore cannot know whether the menu is open or closed. In addition, the trigger’s initial state is never set on page load.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle does not update aria‑expanded on its trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu system (`dropdownMenu`) adds `aria‑haspopup` to each trigger but never updates `aria‑expanded` when a panel is opened or closed. This leaves assistive technologies unaware of the dropdown’s state. The initial `aria‑expanded` value is also missing.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown trigger button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that toggles a dropdown menu (identified by `[data-dropdown]` or `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"]`) receives `aria-controls` and `aria-haspopup` but never gets an `aria-expanded` attribute, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the associated panel is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` when the trigger is initialized and update it to “true”/“false” in the open/close functions.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation toggle button missing aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The hamburger button (`.nav-toggle`) that opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer is wired for click and key events but never receives an `aria-expanded` attribute, leaving its expanded state undisclosed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: In `mobileNav.init` (or when the button is first referenced) set `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false')` and toggle it to “true” when the drawer opens and back to “false” when it closes.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period toggle switch missing aria‑checked
issuesFound.explanation: The billing‑period toggle (`[data-billing-toggle]`) is given `role="switch"` for non‑button elements but never receives an `aria-checked` state, so its on/off status is not communicated to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: After assigning the role, set `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', 'false')` initially and update it to “true”/“false” whenever the period changes (e.g., in `pricing.setPeriod`).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown triggers lack initial aria‑expanded value
issuesFound.explanation: During initialization each dropdown trigger is given `aria‑haspopup="true"` but never receives an explicit `aria‑expanded="false"` reflecting its closed state. Without this, screen readers may assume the menu is already expanded.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The period toggle buttons (`[data-period]`) are visually toggled with a CSS class, but their ARIA state (`aria‑pressed`) is never updated, leaving assistive technology unaware of the current selection.

issuesFound.title: `srUtils.markInvalid` does not set `aria‑invalid` on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When a field fails validation the helper only adds an `aria‑describedby` reference. WCAG requires the field itself to expose its error state via `aria‑invalid="true"` so AT can announce the problem.

issuesFound.title: `srUtils.markValid` does not clear `aria‑invalid` on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: After an error is corrected the helper removes the descriptive reference but leaves `aria‑invalid="true"` on the field, causing AT to continue announcing an error that no longer exists.

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change is not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Switching the pricing period updates numbers and labels, but no ARIA live region is used, so users of assistive technology receive no indication that the prices have changed.

fpTitles: Billing‑period buttons do not update aria‑pressed

issuesFound.title: FormGroup label missing htmlFor

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible rule on line 382 sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative focus indicator for keyboard users. This affects all interactive elements using the .btn class and similar patterns, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible (Level AA) and 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (Level AA).

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum) requirement of 24px × 24px for touch targets. This affects users with motor impairments who rely on larger touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum) requirement of 24px × 24px for touch targets. This affects users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle class sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px. While 28px meets the minimum, it does not meet the recommended 44px × 44px target size for optimal touch usability. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate this target.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a and #nav-menu > li > button selectors set min-height: 28px, which is below the recommended 44px × 44px target size for optimal touch usability. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these targets.

issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the recommended 44px × 44px target size for optimal touch usability. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum) requirement of 24px × 24px and the recommended 44px × 44px. This affects users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector does not set an explicit height or min-height, but the default line height and padding may result in a touch target below 44px × 44px. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .filter-tabs button
issuesFound.explanation: The .filter-tabs button selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the recommended 44px × 44px target size for optimal touch usability. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .pricing-toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-toggle button selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the recommended 44px × 44px target size for optimal touch usability. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .faq-item dt button
issuesFound.explanation: The .faq-item dt button selector sets min-height: 60px, which meets the minimum but the touch target area may not be fully utilized due to padding distribution. Ensure the entire area is interactive for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the `nav-open` class on the body and the `is-open` class on the drawer but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state change, blocking keyboard and screen reader users from understanding whether the navigation is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10014: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
    Line 10020: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-pressed on toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in the `pricing` module toggles the billing period between "month" and "year" and updates the UI but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the pressed state is not programmatically exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 12205: `setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, String(next === 'year'));`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module change the navigation state but do not announce the change to screen readers via a live region. Users who rely on screen readers are not informed when the navigation menu opens or closes, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10014: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
    Line 10020: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `validateField` function in the `inlineValidator` module returns a validation result and updates the error message but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field when validation fails. This violates WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification) as screen readers cannot programmatically identify invalid fields.

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module clears the error message and removes the error class when a field is valid but does not set `aria-invalid="false"` or remove the `aria-invalid` attribute. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the invalid state is not programmatically cleared.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `mobileNav` module does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger button (`_trigger`) when the navigation is closed on page load. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the initial state is not programmatically exposed to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10050: Add after `_trigger` is assigned:
```
setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');
```

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onTriggerClick` function in the `mobileNav` module handles click events on the trigger button (`_trigger`) but there is no corresponding keydown handler for Enter or Space keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) as keyboard users cannot activate the navigation toggle.

issuesFound.title: Content injected via innerHTML without adjacent ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `highlightText` function in the `stringUtils` module injects `<mark>` elements into text using `innerHTML` but does not update an adjacent ARIA live region to announce the highlighted matches to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 as dynamic content changes are not announced.

issuesFound.title: setTimeout changing visible content without pause/stop control
issuesFound.explanation: The `_scheduleNext` function in the `testimonialsCarousel` module uses `setTimeout` to auto-advance slides but does not provide a user-controllable pause/stop mechanism. This violates WCAG 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide) as users cannot pause the carousel.

issuesFound.title: focus() on modal open missing
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the `mobileNav` module opens the navigation drawer but does not programmatically move focus into the drawer. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) as keyboard users are left at the trigger button and must manually navigate into the drawer.

issuesFound.title: focus return on modal close missing
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in the `mobileNav` module closes the navigation drawer but does not return focus to the trigger button (`_trigger`). This violates WCAG 2.4.3 as keyboard users are left without a clear focus location after closing the drawer.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10020: Add after `_unlockScroll();`:
```
if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();
```

issuesFound.title: addEventListener("click") on non-<button>/<a> without keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onDotsClick` function in the `testimonialsCarousel` module adds a click event listener to dot buttons (`[role="tab"]`) but does not add a corresponding keydown handler for Enter or Space keys. This violates WCAG 2.1.1 as keyboard users cannot activate the dot navigation.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing role and keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `testimonialsCarousel` binds click handlers to previous/next buttons but does not ensure these buttons have an appropriate `role` (e.g., `button`) or keyboard handlers for `keydown` events (Enter/Space). This violates WCAG 2.1.1 and 4.1.2.

issuesFound.title: setTimeout changing visible content without pause/stop control

issuesFound.fix: Line 10350: This is a minor cosmetic transition; no pause/stop control is required as it does not affect functionality. No fix needed.

issuesFound.title: Focus not managed on modal open

issuesFound.title: Focus not returned to trigger on modal close
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in `mobileNav` closes the navigation menu but does not return focus to the trigger button (`_trigger`). This violates WCAG 2.4.3 as keyboard users are left without a clear focus location.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10320: Add after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`:
```
    if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();
```

issuesFound.title: Click handler on non-native element without matching keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onTriggerClick` function in `mobileNav` handles click events on the trigger button but does not provide a matching keyboard handler for `keydown` events (Enter/Space). While Issue 15 addresses the missing keyboard handler, this issue confirms the pattern: the click handler exists without a keyboard equivalent.
issuesFound.fix: Already addressed in Issue 15.

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load for accordion items
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `faqAccordion` sets the initial `aria-expanded` state based on `shouldBeOpen` but does not ensure that all trigger buttons have an `aria-expanded` attribute. If `shouldBeOpen` is `false`, the attribute is not set, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Already addressed in Issue 14.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `_validateAll` function in `contactForm` identifies invalid fields and renders an error summary but does not announce the errors to screen readers via a live region. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 as users are not informed of validation errors.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12250: Add after `_renderErrorSummary(errors);`:
```
    liveRegion.announceAssertive(
      (STRINGS.formHasErrors || 'The form contains {n} error{s}. Please review and correct them.')
        .replace('{n}', String(errors.length))
        .replace('{s}', errors.length !== 1 ? 's' : '')
    );
```

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the `is-open` class and update the DOM but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`_trigger`). This prevents screen readers from announcing the state change, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10501: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`
    Line 10505: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`
    Line 10509: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, _open ? 'true' : 'false');` after `_open = !_open;`

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `mobileNav` module does not set an initial `aria-expanded="false"` state on the trigger button (`_trigger`) when the navigation drawer is closed on page load. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) by not providing an initial accessible state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10490: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module change the navigation state but do not announce the change to screen readers via a live region. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) by not providing an accessible announcement of the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10501: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
    Line 10505: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_validateAll` function in the `contactForm` module identifies invalid fields and renders error messages but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid fields. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) by not providing an accessible indication of the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12201: Add inside the `errors.push` block:
    ```
    field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');
    ```

issuesFound.title: aria-invalid not cleared on valid input
issuesFound.explanation: The `validate` function in the `inlineValidator` module sets `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid fields but does not reset it to `false` or remove it when the field becomes valid. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) by leaving an incorrect accessible state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12101: Add inside the `if (result.valid)` block:
    ```
    field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onToggleClick` function in the `pricing` module toggles the billing period but does not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) by not providing an accessible state change announcement.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10901: Add after `const next = _pricingState.period === 'year' ? 'month' : 'year';`:
    ```
    setAttr(_toggleEl, ARIA.PRESSED, String(next === 'year'));
    ```

issuesFound.title: aria-pressed not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `pricing` module does not set an initial `aria-pressed` state on the toggle button (`_toggleEl`) or the period buttons (`_periodBtns`). This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) by not providing an initial accessible state.

issuesFound.title: Non-native interactive element missing keyboard handler
issuesFound.explanation: The `_onTriggerClick` function in the `mobileNav` module handles click events on the trigger button (`_trigger`) but does not provide a corresponding keyboard handler for `keydown` events (Enter/Space). This violates WCAG 2.1.1 (Keyboard) by not ensuring keyboard operability.

issuesFound.title: Content injected via innerHTML without adjacent ARIA live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `render` function in the `suggestionListRenderer` module injects HTML content into the listbox using `innerHTML` but does not update an adjacent ARIA live region to announce the change to screen readers. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) by not providing an accessible announcement of the dynamic content change.

issuesFound.title: setTimeout changing visible content without pause/stop control
issuesFound.explanation: The `play` function in the `testimonialsCarousel` module uses `setTimeout` to auto-advance slides but does not provide a user-controllable pause/stop mechanism. This violates WCAG 2.2.2 (Pause, Stop, Hide) by not allowing users to pause auto-rotating content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11150: The module already provides a play/pause button (`_playBtn`) and pause-on-hover/focus behavior, which satisfies the requirement. No code change is needed, but ensure the button is always visible and accessible.

issuesFound.title: focus() on modal open missing
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in the `mobileNav` module opens the navigation drawer but does not programmatically move focus into the drawer. This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) by not ensuring a logical focus order when a modal-like component opens.

issuesFound.title: focus return on modal close missing
issuesFound.explanation: The `close` function in the `mobileNav` module closes the navigation drawer but does not return focus to the trigger button (`_trigger`). This violates WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) by not restoring focus to the element that opened the modal-like component.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10505: Add after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`:
    ```
    if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing accessible name on interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `mobileNav` module initializes the trigger button (`_trigger`) but does not ensure it has an accessible name. The button uses an icon (hamburger) without a text label or `aria-label`. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value) by not providing an accessible name.

issuesFound.title: Missing autocomplete attribute on personal data input
issuesFound.explanation: The `CONTACT_FIELDS` array in the `contactForm` module defines fields for "full_name", "email", "subject", "message", and "consent" but does not include the `autocomplete` attribute on the corresponding inputs. This violates WCAG 1.3.5 (Identify Input Purpose) by not programmatically identifying the purpose of personal data inputs.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12001: Add to the field configuration for `[name="full_name"]`:
    ```
    autocomplete: 'name',
    ```
    Line 12002: Add to the field configuration for `[name="email"]`:
    ```
    autocomplete: 'email',
    ```
    Line 12003: Add to the field configuration for `[name="subject"]`:
    ```
    // No standard autocomplete value for subject; skip or use custom.
    ```
    Line 12004: Add to the field configuration for `[name="message"]`:
    ```
    // No standard autocomplete value for message; skip or use custom.
    ```
    Line 12005: Add to the field configuration for `[name="consent"]`:
    ```
    // No standard autocomplete value for consent; skip.
    ```

issuesFound.title: Broken aria-controls reference
issuesFound.explanation: The `register` function in the `dropdownMenu` module sets `aria-controls` on the trigger button to the panel's id, but it does not ensure the panel has an id if it is missing. This can result in a broken `aria-controls` reference, violating WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10650: Replace the existing `srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel');` with:
    ```
    const panelId = srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Broken aria-labelledby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The `parse` function in the `faqRegistry` module sets `aria-labelledby` on the panel to the trigger's id, but it does not ensure the trigger has an id if it is missing. This can result in a broken `aria-labelledby` reference, violating WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 11450: Replace the existing `srUtils.ensureId(trigger, 'faq-trigger');` with:
    ```
    const triggerId = srUtils.ensureId(trigger, 'faq-trigger');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-required on required input
issuesFound.explanation: The `CONTACT_FIELDS` array in the `contactForm` module defines several required fields but does not set `aria-required="true"` on the corresponding inputs. While the HTML `required` attribute is present, `aria-required` is also recommended for broader assistive technology support. This violates best practices for WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix:
Line 12001: Add to the field configuration for `[name="full_name"]`:
    ```
    'aria-required': 'true',
    ```
    Line 12002: Add to the field configuration for `[name="email"]`:
    ```
    'aria-required': 'true',
    ```
    Line 12003: Add to the field configuration for `[name="subject"]`:
    ```
    'aria-required': 'true',
    ```
    Line 12004: Add to the field configuration for `[name="message"]`:
    ```
    'aria-required': 'true',
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing fieldset and legend for grouped inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `pricing` module initializes billing period buttons (`_periodBtns`) that act as a group of related radio-like inputs but does not wrap them in a `<fieldset>` with a `<legend>`. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) by not programmatically grouping related form controls.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 10910: (Fix requires changes in external HTML) Wrap the period buttons in a `<fieldset>` and add a `<legend>`:
    ```
    <fieldset>
      <legend>Billing period</legend>
      <!-- buttons here -->
    </fieldset>
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-live region for dynamic content
issuesFound.explanation: The `liveRegion` module is used throughout the codebase to announce dynamic content changes, but its initialization is lazy and may not be present when early dynamic content updates occur. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) by not ensuring a live region is available for announcements.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="status" on status message element
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `newsletterForm` module creates a status element (`_statusEl`) but does not set `role="status"` on it. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) by not programmatically identifying the element as a status message.

issuesFound.title: Missing role="alert" on error message element
issuesFound.explanation: The `_getOrCreateErrorEl` function in the `inlineValidator` module creates an error message element but does not set `role="alert"` on it. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) by not programmatically identifying the element as an error message.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on landmark region
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `mobileNav` module initializes the navigation drawer (`_drawer`) but does not ensure it has an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.6 (Identify Purpose) by not providing an accessible name for the landmark region.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on listbox
issuesFound.explanation: The `mount` function in the `suggestionListRenderer` module creates a listbox element but does not set an `aria-label` on it. This violates WCAG 1.3.6 (Identify Purpose) by not providing an accessible name for the listbox.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on progressbar
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `multiStepWizard` module initializes a progress bar (`_progressBar`) but does not ensure it has an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attribute. This violates WCAG 1.3.6 (Identify Purpose) by not providing an accessible name for the progressbar.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on tablist
issuesFound.explanation: The `register` function in the `dropdownMenu` module initializes a dots container (`_dotsEl`) with `role="tablist"` but does not set an `aria-label` on it. This violates WCAG 1.3.6 (Identify Purpose) by not providing an accessible name for the tablist.
issuesFound.fix: Line 10601: (Fix requires changes in external HTML) Add `aria-label="Select testimonial"` to the dots container.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on combobox
issuesFound.explanation: The `mount` function in the `suggestionListRenderer` module sets `role="combobox"` on the search input but does not set an `aria-label` on it. This violates WCAG 1.3.6 (Identify Purpose) by not providing an accessible name for the combobox.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-label on search landmark
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `siteSearch` module initializes a search form (`_formEl`) but does not ensure it has `role="search"` and an `aria-label`. This violates WCAG 1.3.6 (Identify Purpose) by not providing an accessible name for the search landmark.

issuesFound.title: Missing lang attribute on page
issuesFound.explanation: The `<html>` element does not have a `lang` attribute set in the code. While the `CONFIG.app.locale` is set to 'en-GB', it is not applied to the document. This violates WCAG 3.1.1 (Language of Page) by not programmatically identifying the primary language of the page.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 100: Add to the end of the `app.init` function:
    ```
    document.documentElement.setAttribute('lang', CONFIG.app.locale || 'en-GB');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing title element on page
issuesFound.explanation: The code does not ensure the document has a `<title>` element. While the title may be set in the HTML, the JavaScript does not verify or set it. This violates WCAG 2.4.2 (Page Titled) by not providing a descriptive title for the page.

issuesFound.title: Missing main landmark
issuesFound.explanation: The code does not ensure the document has a `<main>` element with `role="main"`. While the `SELECTORS.main` is defined, the JavaScript does not verify or create it. This violates WCAG 1.3.6 (Identify Purpose) by not providing a main landmark.

issuesFound.title: Missing skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The code does not ensure the document has a skip link. While the `skipLinkManager` is initialized, it relies on the skip link being present in the HTML. This violates WCAG 2.4.1 (Bypass Blocks) by not providing a mechanism to bypass repetitive content.

issuesFound.title: Missing heading hierarchy
issuesFound.explanation: The code does not verify or enforce a logical heading hierarchy (h1 > h2 > h3, etc.) on the page. This violates WCAG 1.3.1 (Info and Relationships) by not providing a clear content structure.
issuesFound.fix: Line 100: (Fix requires changes in external HTML) Ensure the page has a single `<h1>` and a logical heading hierarchy. No code change is possible without HTML context.

issuesFound.title: Missing alt text on decorative image
issuesFound.explanation: The code does not ensure that decorative images have empty `alt` attributes (`alt=""`). While `srUtils.auditImages` is called, it does not enforce this rule. This violates WCAG 1.1.1 (Non-text Content) by not programmatically identifying decorative images.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-hidden on decorative icon
issuesFound.explanation: The `register` function in the `dropdownMenu` module initializes dropdown triggers that may contain decorative icons, but it does not ensure the icons have `aria-hidden="true"`. This violates WCAG 1.1.1 (Non-text Content) by not hiding decorative icons from assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Redundant ARIA role on native element
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `mobileNav` module sets `role="button"` on the trigger button (`_trigger`) even if it is a native `<button>` element. This violates best practices by adding a redundant ARIA role.

issuesFound.title: Missing focus indicator on interactive element
issuesFound.explanation: The code does not ensure that all interactive elements have a visible focus indicator. While `keyboardNavigationGuard` is used, it does not guarantee visible focus styles. This violates WCAG 2.4.7 (Focus Visible) by not providing a visible indication of keyboard focus.

issuesFound.title: Missing touch target size
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `mobileNav` module initializes the trigger button (`_trigger`) but does not ensure it meets the minimum touch target size of 44x44 pixels. This violates WCAG 2.5.8 (Target Size) by not providing a sufficiently large touch target.

issuesFound.title: Missing reduced motion guard on animation
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module animate the navigation drawer but do not check for `prefers-reduced-motion`. This violates WCAG 2.3.3 (Animation from Interactions) by not respecting user motion preferences.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-live region for form submission status
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `newsletterForm` module initializes a form but does not ensure there is an `aria-live` region for announcing submission status. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) by not providing an accessible announcement of the submission result.
issuesFound.fix: Line 11901: The module already creates `_statusEl` with `aria-live="polite"`, which is correct. No change needed.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-describedby on input with error message
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module links the error message to the input using `srUtils.markInvalid`, which sets `aria-describedby`, but it does not ensure the error message element has an `id` if it is missing. This can result in a broken `aria-describedby` reference.

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-atomic on live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `liveRegion` module creates live regions with `aria-live` but does not set `aria-atomic="true"` on the assertive region. This violates best practices for live regions by not ensuring the entire message is read when updated.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 9601: Add to the assertive region creation:
    ```
    assertRegion.setAttribute('aria-atomic', 'true');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-relevant on live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in the `liveRegion` module creates live regions with `aria-live` but does not set `aria-relevant` on the polite region. This violates best practices for live regions by not specifying what types of changes should be announced.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 9601: Add to the polite region creation:
    ```
    politeRegion.wrapper.setAttribute('aria-relevant', 'additions text');
    ```

issuesFound.title: Missing aria-busy on loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The `_fetchAndRender` function in the `siteSearch` module sets a loading state but does not set `aria-busy="true"` on the search form or input. This violates WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages) by not providing an accessible indication of the loading state.

issuesFound.title: Form field missing accessible label
issuesFound.explanation: The SearchInput component's TextInput does not have an explicit accessible label. While it has a placeholder and an aria-label, the label element is visually hidden and may not be sufficient for all screen readers. The input should have a visible label or a properly associated aria-label.

issuesFound.title: Toggle button in SiteHeader missing aria-expanded prop

issuesFound.title: Toggle button in ThemeToggle missing aria-label reflecting current state

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class fails to hide content

This breaks the intended accessibility pattern for screen-reader-only text.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override (.btn)

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override (.skip-link)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.skip-link` selector uses `transition: transform ...` which causes spatial movement. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule disabling this transform for this selector.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override (.hero-ctas .btn-primary)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-ctas .btn-primary` selector uses `transition: transform ...` which causes spatial movement. There is no `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` rule disabling this transform for this selector.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override (.hero-image img)
issuesFound.explanation: The `.hero-image img` selector uses `transition: transform ...` which causes spatial movement. The existing `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block for this selector is empty and does not disable the transform.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links (Account navigation)

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden heading utility implemented correctly but .sr-only is broken

This contradicts the intended use described in comments and breaks accessibility for any element using this class.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open(), close(), and init() functions toggle the navigation visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the _trigger button. Screen reader users will not know if the menu is open or closed.

issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu module's _openInstance(), _closeInstance(), and register() functions toggle the panel visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the inst.trigger button. This breaks the disclosure pattern for screen readers.

issuesFound.title: pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons do not update aria-pressed

issuesFound.title: Global focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class removes the default browser outline without providing a visible replacement style such as box-shadow or border. This prevents keyboard users from seeing which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7 and 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Screen-reader-only utility class fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class incorrectly uses position: static, width: auto, and overflow: visible, which displays content to sighted users instead of hiding it visually. This breaks the utility's purpose of providing content only for assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger buttons missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions manage the visibility of dropdown panels by setting the hidden attribute and toggling CSS classes, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger element (inst.trigger) to communicate the expanded/collapsed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1538: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside _openInstance after `inst.open = true;`. Line 1550: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside _closeInstance after `inst.open = false;`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function calls srUtils.markInvalid() when validation fails, but markInvalid() only manages the aria-describedby attribute linking to the error message. Neither function sets aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Conversely, when validation passes, aria-invalid is not reset to "false". Screen readers cannot identify invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3387: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside srUtils.markInvalid. Line 3402: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` inside srUtils.markValid (or ensure it is removed/cleared).

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count updates not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When productFilter.applyFilters() updates the visible product count via _updateCountBadge(), it updates the text content of the count badge but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not notified of how many results match their filter selection, which is critical feedback for search/filter operations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2736: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);` after updating the text content in _updateCountBadge.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open and close functions add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the drawer, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the visibility state. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1302: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the open function. Line 1318: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside the close function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu instance open and close functions manage the panel visibility by adding or removing the 'hidden' attribute and CSS classes, but they do not synchronise the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware of the panel's visibility state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1455: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside _openInstance. Line 1465: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside _closeInstance.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/quarterly/yearly) visually indicate the active state via CSS classes, but the function does not update aria-pressed to reflect which button is currently pressed. Screen readers cannot convey the selected billing period.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2854: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop in _syncToggleState.

issuesFound.title: markInvalid does not set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as invalid, the function adds an error message and links it via aria-describedby, but it fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2053: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` inside markInvalid.

issuesFound.title: markValid does not clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as valid, the function removes the error message from aria-describedby, but it does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute. The field remains marked as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2082: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` inside markValid.

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched (e.g., monthly to annual), the price values update visually and the toggle state changes, but no announcement is made to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed that prices have changed or what the new billing period is.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2818: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingPeriodChanged || 'Prices switched to ' + period + ' billing');` after updating the UI.

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product count badge is updated with the new count, but this change is not announced to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technologies are not informed how many results match their current filter selection.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3204: Add `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);` after updating the badge text.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. Since the menu starts closed, the trigger should indicate this state immediately so screen readers know the initial state before any interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1388: After `_initialised = true;`, add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input combobox is not initialised with aria-expanded="false" when the page loads. The suggestion list starts hidden, so the input should indicate this collapsed state to assistive technologies immediately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2315: After mounting the listbox, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open function adds CSS classes to show the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the menu is now expanded and available.
issuesFound.fix: Line: _open = true; → Line: _open = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The close function removes CSS classes and hides the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers with stale state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line: _open = false; → Line: _open = false; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the page loads, the mobile navigation trigger button lacks aria-expanded="false", violating the requirement that ARIA state attributes reflect the current state immediately on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line: _initialised = true; → Line: _initialised = true; setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap instantiation
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never instantiates createFocusTrap(). When the drawer opens, keyboard focus is not confined to the drawer, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line: _initialised = true; → Line: _initialised = true; _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: After opening the drawer, the code checks if _trap is active but never activates it. Focus remains free-roaming instead of being trapped within the navigation drawer.
issuesFound.fix: Line: _lockScroll(); → Line: _lockScroll(); if (_trap) _trap.activate();

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown open function reveals the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers unaware that the menu is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line: inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); → Line: inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown close function hides the panel but does not set aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button, causing screen readers to report the menu as still expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line: inst.open = false; → Line: inst.open = false; setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu.register does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a new dropdown pair, the trigger button is not given aria-expanded="false" to reflect its initial closed state on page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line: panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); → Line: panel.setAttribute('hidden', ''); setAttr(trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the search suggestion panel opens, the combobox input does not receive aria-expanded="true", so screen readers do not announce that the listbox is now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line: _searchState.open = true; → Line: _searchState.open = true; setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._close does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When the search suggestion panel closes, aria-expanded remains stale. The input lacks aria-expanded="false" to indicate the listbox is hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line: _searchState.open = false; → Line: _searchState.open = false; setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch.init does not initialise aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is converted to a combobox but aria-expanded is not initialised to "false" on page load, violating the requirement for immediate state reflection.
issuesFound.fix: Line: _initialised = true; → Line: _initialised = true; setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers with incorrect collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); → Line: item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden'); setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons act as toggle buttons but only CSS classes are updated; aria-pressed is never set to reflect which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line: toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); → Line: toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: pricing._syncToggleState does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: Billing period toggle buttons indicate active state via CSS only; aria-pressed is never updated to communicate the pressed state to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line: toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); → Line: toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive); setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the function marks the field visually and links the error message but never sets aria-invalid="true", so screen readers do not announce the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line: addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); → Line: addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult does not clear aria-invalid on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When validation passes, aria-invalid is not reset to "false" or removed, leaving screen readers with stale invalid state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line: addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid); → Line: addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid); setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');

issuesFound.title: Required field marker announces bare asterisk to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The required field asterisk (*) is visible to screen readers as a bare character. While there is a visually hidden "(required)" label, the asterisk itself should be hidden to avoid redundant or confusing announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 549-553: ```tsx {required && ( <> * (required) )} ```

issuesFound.title: Loading button missing aria-busy state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component does not communicate its loading state via aria-busy when isLoading is true. Screen reader users may not be aware that the button is in a busy/loading state.

issuesFound.title: Decorative spinner ring not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner component's visual ring element is decorative but lacks aria-hidden. Screen readers may announce the inner span element redundantly alongside the parent's role="status" and aria-label.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 500-503:
```tsx
<span className="spinner__ring" aria-hidden="true" />
```

issuesFound.title: Decorative button spinner not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: When the Button component is in a loading state, the spinner span is decorative (the loading state is announced via VisuallyHidden) but lacks aria-hidden. Screen readers may announce the spinner element redundantly.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 433-437:
```tsx
{isLoading && (
  <>
    <span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />
    <VisuallyHidden>{loadingLabel}</VisuallyHidden>
  </>
)}
```

issuesFound.title: Decorative star characters not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The StarRating component renders star characters (★/☆) that are decorative since the parent span already provides an aria-label with the full rating context. These characters should be hidden from screen readers to avoid redundant announcement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 489-492: ```tsx {filled ? '★' : '☆'} ```
