issuesFound.title: MobileNav close button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The close button in MobileNav uses IconButton with only a visual "✕" symbol but does not pass an aria-label prop. The IconButton component requires aria-label for icon-only buttons. Without it, screen reader users hear no meaningful name for the close button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 752: `<IconButton aria-label="Close navigation menu" onClick={onClose} className="mobile-nav__close">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The previous button in TestimonialsCarousel uses IconButton with only a "‹" symbol but does not pass an aria-label prop. The IconButton component requires aria-label for icon-only buttons. Screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1521: `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The next button in TestimonialsCarousel uses IconButton with only a "›" symbol but does not pass an aria-label prop. The IconButton component requires aria-label for icon-only buttons. Screen reader users cannot identify the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1526: `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">`

issuesFound.title: Multiple navigation landmarks lack distinguishing accessible names
issuesFound.explanation: The page contains multiple navigation regions (SkipLinks nav, DesktopNav nav, and MobileNav nav) but none have aria-label or aria-labelledby to distinguish them. When multiple landmarks of the same type exist, each requires a unique accessible name so screen reader users can differentiate between them.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 60: `<nav aria-label="Skip navigation links">`
  Fix: Line 695: `<nav id="main-nav" aria-label="Main navigation" className="desktop-nav">`
  Fix: Line 765: `<nav aria-label="Mobile navigation" className="mobile-nav__body">`

issuesFound.title: Button component missing aria-busy during loading state
issuesFound.explanation: The Button component renders a loading spinner and disables the button when isLoading is true, but does not expose the busy state via aria-busy. Screen reader users are not informed that the button is currently processing.
issuesFound.fix: Line 511: `<button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading ? 'true' : undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest}>`

issuesFound.title: Button loading spinner missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The loading spinner span inside Button is decorative visual content that duplicates the accessible loading announcement provided by VisuallyHidden. Without aria-hidden="true", screen readers may announce meaningless content for the spinner element.
issuesFound.fix: Line 519: `<span className="btn-spinner" aria-hidden="true" />`

issuesFound.title: Spinner component ring element missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The spinner__ring span is a decorative visual element inside a component that already has role="status" and aria-label. Without aria-hidden="true", the ring element may be exposed to assistive technologies as meaningless content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 550: `<span className="spinner__ring" aria-hidden="true" />`

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links with `item.current === true` receive a CSS class indicating the current page but do not expose this state programmatically via aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 706: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current page link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Mobile navigation links with `item.current === true` receive a CSS class indicating the current page but do not expose this state programmatically via aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which link represents the current page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 791: `<a href={item.href} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined} className={['mobile-nav__link', item.current ? 'mobile-nav__link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={onClose}>`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel inactive slides missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Carousel slides that are not currently visible remain in the DOM without aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users may navigate to and hear content from non-visible slides, creating confusion about which testimonial is actually being displayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1502: `<div key={t.id} role="group" aria-roledescription="slide" aria-label={`Quote ${i + 1} of ${total}`} aria-hidden={!isVisible ? 'true' : undefined} className={['testimonial-slide', isVisible ? 'testimonial-slide--visible' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')}>`

issuesFound.title: Required field marker asterisk not hidden from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The required field marker renders a visible asterisk (*) with class "form-required-marker" but does not have aria-hidden="true". Screen readers will announce the asterisk character redundantly alongside the VisuallyHidden "(required)" text, creating duplicate announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 597: ` *`
